title: cyclamen peloponnesiacum subsp. rhodense
intro: Cyclamen peloponnesiacum subsp. rhodense is a rare, endangered species of flowering plant within the cyclamen genus, native to Greece. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant abilities. The health benefits of Cyclamen peloponnesiacum subsp. rhodense include relief from colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and have been used in folk medicine for centuries, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
